---
bibliography: ../references.bib
---

# Discussion

## Model Interpretation and Stock Status

The interpretation of the selected `r species_label` model should consider the
diagnostic evidence, data conflicts, sensitivity runs, structural uncertainty,
and implications for stock-status advice.

> **TODO:** Add the final interpretation after the diagnostic model and
> management-advice grid are accepted.

## Comparison with the Previous Assessment

The comparison with the `r previous_assessment_year` assessment should identify
which changes are caused by new data, model structure, biological assumptions,
CPUE standardisation, tagging treatment, model software, and convergence or
uncertainty treatment.

The first comparison pass should separate changes in the data time series from
changes in assumptions. The `r previous_assessment_year` assessment introduced
or emphasised the following items that should be checked explicitly:
catch-conditioned modelling and index-fishery CPUE likelihood; sdmTMB CPUE
standardisation and region-specific CPUE variances; internal growth estimation
from conditional age-at-length data; Lorenzen natural mortality; revised
tagger-effect and tag-reporting treatments; size-composition filtering and
weighting; selectivity penalty settings; and extensive jitter/Hessian
screening of diagnostic and grid models.

> **TODO:** Compare against the previous assessment only after equivalent
> quantities are recomputed from the accepted `r assessment_year` model set.

## Remaining Uncertainties

Remaining uncertainties should be separated into data limitations, model
structure, biological assumptions, tagging assumptions, abundance-index
uncertainty, and computational or convergence limitations.

Reusable uncertainty themes from the previous assessment include the importance
of representative biological sampling, the sensitivity of tag-based models to
tag mixing and reporting assumptions, the influence of size-composition data
filtering and weighting, and the need to diagnose convergence carefully in
complex integrated models. These themes should be retained only where the
current diagnostics support them.

The previous assessment also noted that apparently well-converged local
solutions could be improved through jittering, sometimes with meaningful
changes in derived management quantities. For the current report, uncertainty
statements should therefore distinguish between structural uncertainty,
estimation uncertainty, and optimisation uncertainty. Where models have
non-positive-definite Hessians or small negative eigenvalues, the text should
explain whether key management quantities remain stable and whether estimation
uncertainty was still calculated.

> **TODO:** Replace with the final `r assessment_year` uncertainty discussion.

## Research Recommendations

> **TODO:** Update recommendations from the `r assessment_year` assessment.
> Reuse previous recommendations only where they remain directly supported by
> the current diagnostics and data review.

Potential carry-forward recommendations from the previous assessment include
continued representative biological sampling, especially age data; sustained
technical capacity for software, data preparation, and quality control; enough
assessment time and computing resources to explore complex integrated models;
continued collection of representative tagging data; and comprehensive review
of size-composition data. These should be edited or removed after the
`r assessment_year` diagnostics show which data sources and assumptions are
most limiting.
